evals.prompt: I want to create a new skill for generating API documentation from OpenAPI specs. The skill should read OpenAPI YAML files and produce comprehensive documentation with examples. Can you help me build this skill?
evals.expected_output: Agent should follow the skill-creator workflow: 1) Interview to understand requirements (target audience, output format, frameworks), 2) Create SKILL.md with clear workflow and instructions, 3) Create evals/evals.json with test cases, 4) Create reference documentation if needed, 5) Set up proper file structure.
expectations: Interview questions asked to gather requirements
SKILL.md created with clear workflow steps
SKILL.md includes trigger description in frontmatter
evals/evals.json created with at least 2 test cases
File structure follows skill-creator conventions
Reference documentation created if complexity warrants it

evals.prompt: I have an existing design-system skill but it's not triggering reliably. Can you help me improve the skill description and test it?
evals.expected_output: Agent should: 1) Read existing SKILL.md, 2) Analyze trigger description, 3) Suggest improvements to skill description for better triggering, 4) Create or update evals for testing, 5) Run comparison tests to verify improvements.
expectations: Existing SKILL.md read and analyzed
Trigger description identified and reviewed
Specific improvements suggested for trigger optimization
Test cases created or updated in evals/evals.json
Comparison test workflow explained or executed

evals.prompt: I just created a new skill for PDF processing. How do I set up testing for it? I want to make sure it works correctly before deploying.
evals.expected_output: Agent should explain the testing workflow: 1) Create evals/evals.json with test cases, 2) Run static tests (quick_validate.py, static_analysis.py, trigger_readiness.py), 3) Run comparison tests using run_comparison.py, 4) Review results using eval-viewer, 5) Iterate based on findings.
expectations: evals/evals.json structure explained
Static test suite mentioned (no subagents required)
Comparison test workflow explained (with/without skill)
Scripts referenced correctly (run_comparison.py, static_test_suite.py)
Eval viewer mentioned for reviewing results
File paths and commands are accurate

evals.prompt: My skill-creator skill is complete but I want to optimize its description so it triggers more reliably. Can you help me improve the trigger accuracy?
evals.expected_output: Agent should: 1) Read current SKILL.md description, 2) Generate 20 trigger eval queries (mix of should-trigger and should-not-trigger), 3) Present eval set for user review using HTML template, 4) Export eval set as JSON, 5) Explain trigger optimization based on Cycle 5 research.
expectations: Current skill description analyzed
20 trigger eval queries generated (8-10 should-trigger, 8-10 should-not-trigger)
Eval queries are realistic with personal context and detail
Should-not-trigger queries are near-misses (not obviously irrelevant)
HTML eval review template used with correct placeholders
Eval set exported as JSON to Downloads folder

evals.prompt: I've improved my skill and want to compare the new version against the old one. How do I run a proper benchmark comparison?
evals.expected_output: Agent should: 1) Explain comparison test setup (with-skill vs baseline), 2) Spawn subagents for each test case (with-skill and baseline in same turn), 3) Capture timing data from task notifications, 4) Draft quantitative assertions while runs complete, 5) Grade runs and aggregate into benchmark, 6) Launch eval viewer for user review.
expectations: Comparison test workflow explained clearly
With-skill and baseline runs spawned in same turn
Proper directory structure created (iteration-N/eval-ID/)
Timing data captured to timing.json
Quantitative assertions drafted with descriptive names
Benchmark aggregated using aggregate_benchmark.py script
Eval viewer launched with generate_review.py

evals.prompt: I need to add quantitative evals to my skill. How do I write good assertions that can be automatically verified?
evals.expected_output: Agent should: 1) Explain assertion structure (id, name, type, expected), 2) Show examples of good vs bad assertions, 3) Help draft 3-5 assertions per eval case, 4) Explain assertion types (contains, equals, regex, file-exists), 5) Write grading script for programmatic assertions.
expectations: Assertion schema explained (id, name, type, expected)
Good vs bad assertion examples provided
3-5 assertions drafted per eval case
Assertions use objective verifiable criteria
Grading script created for programmatic checks
evals/evals.json updated with assertions array

evals.prompt: My skill exists but Claude isn't using it. The description says 'Build dashboards' but when I ask for a dashboard, it doesn't trigger. Help me debug this.
evals.expected_output: Agent should: 1) Check skill registration (ls ~/.qwen/skills/), 2) Analyze description for trigger keywords, 3) Identify missing trigger patterns based on Cycle 5 research, 4) Rewrite description with 'I need a...' pattern matching, 5) Add domain keywords and example trigger phrases, 6) Test with explicit need statement.
expectations: Skill registration verified
Current description analyzed for trigger gaps
Cycle 5 research applied (need statement pattern)
Description rewritten with trigger optimization
Domain keywords added (3+ required)
Example trigger phrases included
Test with 'I need a [domain] for my [context]' pattern

evals.prompt: I want to create a skill that generates docx files. The skill should bundle a Python script for docx generation since all test cases need it. How do I structure this?
evals.expected_output: Agent should: 1) Identify repeated work pattern (docx generation), 2) Create scripts/create_docx.py with reusable logic, 3) Reference script from SKILL.md, 4) Set up proper file structure (scripts/, assets/), 5) Include script execution instructions in workflow.
expectations: Repeated work pattern identified across test cases
scripts/create_docx.py created with reusable logic
SKILL.md references script with clear instructions
File structure includes scripts/ directory
Script includes error handling and edge cases
Workflow explains when and how to execute script
